Effective 16 January 2026, Chinese nationals may enter visa-free for up to 14 days (non-extendible, non-convertible) for tourism/business. Entry only through Ninoy Aquino International Airport (Manila) or Mactan-Cebu International Airport. Transit requires a separate Philippine transit visa. Mainland Chinese holding valid Australian, Japanese, Canadian, Schengen or US visa may enter for 7 days (extendable by 14 days, maximum 21 days) under FSC No. 112-11.

Effective June 8, 2025: 14 days visa-free (non-extendible, non-convertible) for tourism/business. Requires passport valid 6+ months, confirmed hotel booking, proof of financial capacity, return/onward ticket. Indian nationals holding valid and current American, Japanese, Australian, Canadian, Schengen, Singapore or United Kingdom (AJACSSUK) visas or residence permits may enter for a non-extendible period of 30 days for tourism. These privileges are not convertible and are available at any Philippine port of entry.

e_visa.notes: Eligible for Philippine e-Visa (9a Temporary Visitor's Visa). Processing: 7-12 working days (single entry), 15-21 working days (multiple entry). Cannot be extended locally.

visa_policy.notes: Visa-free entry governed primarily by Executive Order No. 408. The official evisa.gov.ph policy page lists the complete EO 408 country list. Brazil and Israel receive 59 days; most others receive 30 days. China (effective Jan 16, 2026) and India (effective Jun 8, 2025) receive 14 days under separate arrangements; entry for China is restricted to NAIA (Manila) and Mactan-Cebu International Airport only. Hong Kong SAR and Macau SAR passport holders receive 14 days. The e-Visa program (evisa.gov.ph) covers 9(a) Temporary Visitor's Visa only. Note: several e-visa-eligible nationalities are also visa-free; the e-visa provides an alternative mechanism (59 days vs 30 days). SEVUA (visa upon arrival) is endorsement/purpose-based, not nationality-based. The Philippines has no ETA system as of 2026.

cbi.notes: The Philippines has no citizenship-by-investment program. The country offers investor-based residency (SIRV) but not a pathway to citizenship through investment. The DFA and Bureau of Immigration confirm only residency visas for investors.

programs.name: Special Investor's Resident Visa (SIRV)
programs.notes: Requires remittance of at least USD 75,000 into a Peso Time Deposit at Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) or Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP). Funds must remain on deposit for minimum 30 days, maximum 180 days. Investment must be made within 180 days of probationary visa issuance. Eligible investments: shares of stocks in corporations in BOI Investment Priorities Plan (IPP) areas or manufacturing/services sectors. Probationary SIRV valid 6 months; Indefinite SIRV valid indefinitely while investment maintained. Issued by Bureau of Immigration upon endorsement by Board of Investments. No path to citizenship documented. Minimum age: 21 years. One-Stop SIRV Center at 2nd Floor, BOI, #385 Senator Gil J. Puyat Avenue, Makati City. Source: BOI official FAQ (boi.gov.ph) and DFA consular pages (dfa.gov.ph).

programs.name: Special Resident Retiree's Visa (SRRV)
programs.notes: Issued by Bureau of Immigration under the Philippine Retirement Authority (PRA). Multiple options: SRRV Classic (age 50+, pensioner: USD 15,000; non-pensioner: USD 30,000; age 40-49, pensioner: USD 25,000; non-pensioner: USD 50,000) and SRRV Courtesy for foreign nationals (age 50+, pensioner: USD 1,500; non-pensioner: USD 3,000; age 40-49 non-pensioner: USD 6,000) and for former Filipinos (age 50+: USD 1,500; age 40-49: USD 3,000). Multiple entry, indefinite stay. Holders may retain their original citizenship. No documented path to Philippine citizenship through SRRV. Minimum age: 40 years.

programs.name: Treaty Trader or Treaty Investor Visa (9D)
programs.notes: Available only to nationals of USA, Japan, and Germany under existing treaties of commerce and navigation. Applicant must enter to carry on trade/commerce or to develop/direct operations of an enterprise. No minimum investment amount specified on official page. Renewable visa; 1-year or 2-year validity. No documented path to PR or citizenship.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Visa (DNV)
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals aged 18+ who work remotely via digital technologies exclusively for overseas clients or employers. Must be from countries offering reciprocal digital nomad visas to Filipinos and maintaining a Philippine Foreign Service Post. Minimum income: USD 24,000/year. Must have valid health insurance. Clean criminal record required.
programs.notes: Authorized by Executive Order No. 86, s. 2025 (signed April 24, 2025). Allows stay up to 1 year, renewable for another year. Foreign-sourced income not subject to Philippine taxation. Implementing rules and regulations by Bureau of Immigration were pending as of the last official access date. Application through Bureau of Immigration website or Philippine embassies/consulates once IRR published.

programs.name: Special Visa for Employment Generation (SVEG)
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals whose employment will generate at least 10 jobs for Filipinos. Issued by Bureau of Immigration.
programs.notes: Listed under Special Visas on the Bureau of Immigration website. Specific requirements and processing details not published on official web pages accessed.

agreements: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) - member state, party to ASEAN Movement of Natural Persons (MNP) Agreement for temporary cross-border movement of service providers

conditional_access.conditions: Mainland Chinese nationals traveling as tourists holding a valid or unexpired Australian visa may enter visa-free for an initial stay not exceeding 7 days (FSC No. 112-11). The 7-day stay may be extended by an additional 14 days to complete a maximum of 21 days. Passport must be valid; return/onward ticket required. No derogatory BI records. Note: the plain 14-day visa-free entry is only via NAIA or Mactan-Cebu; the credential-based 7-day rule via FSC No. 112-11 predates and coexists with the January 2026 14-day rule - holders of qualifying credentials therefore get the benefit of either.

Mainland Chinese nationals traveling as tourists holding a valid or unexpired Japanese visa may enter visa-free for an initial stay not exceeding 7 days (FSC No. 112-11). The 7-day stay may be extended by an additional 14 days to complete a maximum of 21 days. Passport must be valid; return/onward ticket required. No derogatory BI records.

Mainland Chinese nationals traveling as tourists holding a valid or unexpired Canadian visa may enter visa-free for an initial stay not exceeding 7 days (FSC No. 112-11). The 7-day stay may be extended by an additional 14 days to complete a maximum of 21 days. Passport must be valid; return/onward ticket required. No derogatory BI records.

Mainland Chinese nationals traveling as tourists holding a valid or unexpired Schengen visa may enter visa-free for an initial stay not exceeding 7 days (FSC No. 112-11). The 7-day stay may be extended by an additional 14 days to complete a maximum of 21 days. Passport must be valid; return/onward ticket required. No derogatory BI records.

Mainland Chinese nationals traveling as tourists holding a valid or unexpired US visa may enter visa-free for an initial stay not exceeding 7 days (FSC No. 112-11). The 7-day stay may be extended by an additional 14 days to complete a maximum of 21 days. Passport must be valid; return/onward ticket required. No derogatory BI records.

credential.subtype: any valid tourist, business or resident visa

conditional_access.conditions: Indian nationals traveling as tourists and holding a valid and current Australian tourist, business or resident visa or residence permit may enter the Philippines without a visa for a non-extendible period of 30 days (AJACSSUK provision, effective June 8, 2025). Valid passport and return/onward ticket required. Admitted from any port of entry.

credential.subtype: any valid tourist, business or resident visa

conditional_access.conditions: Indian nationals traveling as tourists and holding a valid and current Japanese tourist, business or resident visa or residence permit may enter the Philippines without a visa for a non-extendible period of 30 days (AJACSSUK provision, effective June 8, 2025). Valid passport and return/onward ticket required. Admitted from any port of entry.

credential.subtype: any valid tourist, business or resident visa

conditional_access.conditions: Indian nationals traveling as tourists and holding a valid and current American tourist, business or resident visa or residence permit may enter the Philippines without a visa for a non-extendible period of 30 days (AJACSSUK provision, effective June 8, 2025). Valid passport and return/onward ticket required. Admitted from any port of entry.

credential.subtype: any valid tourist, business or resident visa

conditional_access.conditions: Indian nationals traveling as tourists and holding a valid and current Canadian tourist, business or resident visa or residence permit may enter the Philippines without a visa for a non-extendible period of 30 days (AJACSSUK provision, effective June 8, 2025). Valid passport and return/onward ticket required. Admitted from any port of entry.

credential.subtype: any valid tourist, business or resident Schengen visa

conditional_access.conditions: Indian nationals traveling as tourists and holding a valid and current Schengen tourist, business or resident visa or residence permit may enter the Philippines without a visa for a non-extendible period of 30 days (AJACSSUK provision, effective June 8, 2025). Valid passport and return/onward ticket required. Admitted from any port of entry.

credential.subtype: any valid tourist, business or resident visa

conditional_access.conditions: Indian nationals traveling as tourists and holding a valid and current Singapore tourist, business or resident visa or residence permit may enter the Philippines without a visa for a non-extendible period of 30 days (AJACSSUK provision, effective June 8, 2025). Valid passport and return/onward ticket required. Admitted from any port of entry.

credential.subtype: any valid tourist, business or resident visa

conditional_access.conditions: Indian nationals traveling as tourists and holding a valid and current United Kingdom tourist, business or resident visa or residence permit may enter the Philippines without a visa for a non-extendible period of 30 days (AJACSSUK provision, effective June 8, 2025). Valid passport and return/onward ticket required. Admitted from any port of entry.

credential.subtype: Balikbayan status (RA 6768 as amended by RA 9174)

conditional_access.conditions: Balikbayan privilege under RA 6768 as amended by RA 9174. Eligible: (a) Filipino citizens continuously abroad for at least 1 year; (b) Filipino overseas workers; (c) former Filipino citizens naturalized abroad and their families. Immediate family members (spouse and children) of the Balikbayan who are nationals of EO 408 countries and traveling together with the Balikbayan also admitted for 1 year. Non-EO-408 nationals who are spouses/children of Balikbayans are NOT entitled and must secure entry visas. Stay extendable for 1, 2, or 6 months.

credential.subtype: endorsement from BOI/PRA/PCCI or other relevant Philippine government agency or organizer

conditional_access.conditions: Special Visa Upon Arrival (SEVUA) is available to foreign investors endorsed by BOI/PRA/PCCI, athletes, conference delegates, World Bank/ADB officials, and other categories at the discretion of the Immigration Commissioner. Not nationality-based; requires endorsement from relevant Philippine government agency or organizer. Single-entry typically grants 30 days; multiple-entry available for multinational executives.

Taiwanese nationals holding a valid MECO Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) — issued by the Manila Economic and Cultural Office (Taiwan's de facto representative office in the Philippines) — may enter the Philippines visa-free for an initial stay of 30 days under Operations Order No. SBM-2015-018. Taiwan is not listed under EO 408; plain nationality entry without MECO ETA requires a visa. Passport must be valid for at least 6 months; return/onward ticket required.

validation_notes: ADDED Costa Rica (CRI) to visa_free list - present on the live evisa.gov.ph EO 408 page but missing from the previous record.

CORRECTED India AJACSSUK notes: previous record listed 'South Korea' as one of the 6 qualifying credential issuers. The official evisa.gov.ph page specifies American (USA), Japanese, Australian, Canadian, Schengen, Singapore, United Kingdom - 7 issuers, no South Korea. South Korea is NOT in the AJACSSUK provision for India. Added missing USA conditional_access entry for India.

CORRECTED China 14-day visa-free notes: clarified entry is restricted to Ninoy Aquino International Airport (NAIA) and Mactan-Cebu International Airport only. Transit in Philippines still requires a separate transit visa. These restrictions were not clearly stated in the previous record.

CORRECTED China FSC 112-11 credential provision: the official page lists only 5 issuers for the 7-day Chinese credential-based entry (Australia, Japan, Canada, Schengen, US) - no UK or South Korea. Previous record was correct on these 5; verified unchanged.
UPDATED SEVUA max_stay_days from null to 30 (single entry), per the Bureau of Immigration page which specifies 30 days authorized stay for single-entry SEVUA.
CORRECTED India notes field to correctly say AJACSSUK = American, Japanese, Australian, Canadian, Schengen, Singapore, UK (removed South Korea, added USA).
NOTED official portal name for South Korea is 'Republic of Korea' - updated nationality field in visa_free entry.
NOTED official portal uses 'Swaziland' (outdated) for Eswatini - retained iso3 SWZ, noted in the entry.
NOTED official portal uses 'Lao People's Democratic Republic' - updated nationality name from 'Laos'.
NOTED official portal uses 'United Republic of Tanzania' - noted in entry.

e-visa eligible country list: the evisa.gov.ph website renders the nationality list via JavaScript dropdown not accessible as static HTML. The 12 countries in the e_visa section are sourced from prior research citing the official site; could not re-verify the full dynamic list in this validation pass.

2026-06-27 RE-VALIDATION: Re-fetched evisa.gov.ph visa-free policy page and immigration.gov.ph SEVUA page. All 157 EO 408 visa-free countries confirmed present and correct. China 14-day (effective Jan 16, 2026, restricted to NAIA/Mactan-Cebu), India 14-day (effective Jun 8, 2025) with AJACSSUK 30-day credential provision, Brazil/Israel 59-day, Hong Kong SAR/Macau SAR 14-day - all confirmed unchanged. SEVUA terms confirmed (30-day single-entry, endorsement-based). SRRV official pra.gov.ph page shows same deposit amounts as recorded (Classic: USD 15,000 / USD 30,000 age 50+; Courtesy: USD 1,500 / USD 3,000 age 50+). No structural changes to the visa-free, visa_on_arrival, eta, or e_visa lists were found. No new countries added or removed from EO 408 list.

2026-06-29 RE-VALIDATION: Re-fetched evisa.gov.ph policy page, immigration.gov.ph SEVUA page, pra.gov.ph SRRV page, and DFA advisory pages. Key corrections: (1) REMOVED misleading note 'MECO ETA holders: 30 days' from the China (CHN) visa_free entry — this note refers to Taiwanese nationals with MECO ETA, not Chinese nationals. (2) ADDED Taiwan MECO ETA entry to conditional_access: Taiwanese nationals holding a MECO ETA may enter for 30 days under Operations Order No. SBM-2015-018 (credential-based, not plain nationality waiver — Taiwan is not on EO 408). All other data confirmed unchanged: EO 408 list intact, India 14-day standard / 30-day AJACSSUK, China 14-day NAIA/Mactan-Cebu only, SRRV amounts unchanged on live pra.gov.ph page.
gaps: Complete enumeration of e-visa eligible countries: the official evisa.gov.ph site renders the 9(a) e-visa eligible nationality list via a JavaScript dropdown which is not accessible as static content. The 12 countries captured are from prior research; full current list cannot be confirmed from live page scrape.
SIRV dual citizenship policy: the official BOI/BI pages do not explicitly state whether dual citizenship is allowed for SIRV holders; SRRV pages note holders may retain original citizenship.

Digital Nomad Visa implementing rules: EO 86 signed April 2025, took effect May 5, 2025. As of June 2026, IRR status remains unclear - some third-party sources report partial implementation since mid-2025 pilot, others report full IRR not yet finalized. Official immigration.gov.ph page does not yet show a live DNV application portal. Cannot confirm full operational status from official source; gap maintained.

SRRV September 2025 changes: PRA published a document titled 'Processing of SRRV Application (Revised Sep 2025)' visible in search results at pra.gov.ph/Uploads/Files/. The official pra.gov.ph/SRRVisa page retrieved 2026-06-27 and 2026-06-29 still shows the previous amounts (Classic age 50+ pensioner: USD 15,000; Classic age 50+ non-pensioner: USD 30,000; Courtesy age 50+: USD 1,500). The Sep 2025 PDF could not be read via WebFetch (binary/encoded). Third-party sources suggest lower thresholds and abolition of SRRV Smile/Human Touch sub-types, but these cannot be confirmed from live official pages. Existing amounts preserved per official-source-first rule. Gap maintained.

Diplomatic/official/service passport-only visa waivers: multiple DFA.gov.ph bilateral agreement pages returned 403/404. These agreements (Albania, Armenia, Bangladesh, China, Egypt, India, Iran, Jordan, Pakistan, Timor-Leste) are referenced in DFA news but cannot be confirmed from a currently accessible official page, so are not recorded per official-source-first rule.
OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder status: No official Philippine government source found confirming any special entry rule for OCI cardholders.
GCC/UAE residence permit as credential for non-India/non-China nationals: No official Philippine government page found confirming such a rule.
Countries not on EO 408 list that are not eligible for e-visa (requiring embassy visa): specific enumeration not available from sources accessed.

The evisa.gov.ph portal appears to use some outdated country names (Swaziland instead of Eswatini, Eritre instead of Eritrea, Angol instead of Angola) - these are typographical/naming issues on the official portal, not data errors.

Taiwan MECO ETA full scope: The MECO ETA entry (conditional_access) cites Operations Order No. SBM-2015-018. The full text of this order was not accessible via WebFetch to verify all conditions. The 30-day stay period and MECO ETA credential requirement are sourced from the evisa.gov.ph policy page reference and the DFA search result summary. If the operations order has been superseded or amended, this entry may need re-verification against BI or DFA official sources.

outbound_official.note: No official Philippines government outbound visa-list page located. All dfa.gov.ph and consular.dfa.gov.ph pages exclusively cover inbound travel (who may enter the Philippines without a visa under Executive Order No. 408). The Department of Foreign Affairs publishes no equivalent of an origin-side visa-free/visa-on-arrival list for Filipino ordinary-passport holders traveling abroad. All outbound travel access data available online comes from third-party aggregators (Henley Passport Index/IATA, VisaGuide, Atlys, etc.) - none are official Philippine government sources. Rely on destination-side data.

visa_types.name: 9(a-2) Temporary Visitor's Visa – Tourism/Leisure
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals visiting the Philippines temporarily for pleasure, tourism, leisure, religious, recreational, or amusement purposes, or to visit family, relatives, or friends.
visa_types.notes: Issued as Philippine e-Visa via evisa.gov.ph. Single entry: 7-12 working days processing; multiple entry: 15-21 working days. Cannot be extended or converted locally. Passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond departure. Most nationalities under EO 408 are visa-free for 30 days; e-Visa grants up to 59 days. Fee varies by nationality (dynamic pricing on site).

visa_types.name: 9(a-1) Temporary Visitor's Visa – Business
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals conducting temporary business or commercial activities in the Philippines without receiving Philippine-sourced compensation.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the 9(a) Temporary Visitor's Visa. Issued as Philippine e-Visa via evisa.gov.ph. Covers business meetings, conferences, training, and seminars. Cannot be extended or converted locally. Processing: 7-12 working days (single), 15-21 working days (multiple).

visa_types.name: 9(a-3) Temporary Visitor's Visa – Medical Treatment
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals seeking medical treatment or health care in the Philippines, including their escorts.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the 9(a) Temporary Visitor's Visa. Issued as Philippine e-Visa. Cannot be extended or converted locally. Escorts of medical patients are included under this category.

visa_types.name: 9(a-4) Temporary Visitor's Visa – Private Yachtsmen/Sailors
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals making a temporary visit to the Philippines on board a private yacht or sailboat without any employment relationship in the Philippines.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the 9(a) Temporary Visitor's Visa. Covers private maritime visits only; no local employment permitted.

visa_types.name: 9(a-5) Temporary Visitor's Visa – Filmmaking
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals visiting the Philippines for movie or documentary filming purposes as an independent activity.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the 9(a) Temporary Visitor's Visa. No local compensation permitted.

visa_types.name: 9(a-6) Temporary Visitor's Visa – Journalism
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals visiting the Philippines for news and journalism purposes without local employment arrangements.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the 9(a) Temporary Visitor's Visa. No Philippine-sourced compensation permitted.

visa_types.name: 9(a-7) Temporary Visitor's Visa – Athletic and Performing Arts
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals visiting the Philippines for athletic competitions or performing arts engagements without receiving Philippine compensation.
visa_types.notes: Sub-category of the 9(a) Temporary Visitor's Visa. Covers athletes, performers, and entertainers for temporary visits without local employment.

visa_types.name: 9(a-8) Temporary Visitor's Visa – Other Purposes
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals visiting the Philippines temporarily for purposes not covered by other 9(a) sub-categories.
visa_types.notes: Catch-all sub-category of the 9(a) Temporary Visitor's Visa with appropriate supporting documentation required.

visa_types.name: 9(f) Student Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals aged 18 or older who will take up a course of study higher than high school at a Philippine university, seminary, or college.
visa_types.notes: Conversion fee: PHP 9,720 (approximately USD 170 as of 2014 rates) plus USD 50 for ACR I-Card. Extension fee: PHP 4,020 plus USD 50 for ACR I-Card. Applicant must be enrolled at a Philippine Commission on Higher Education (CHED)-accredited institution. Apply at BI Main Office or authorized regional immigration offices. Duration of stay tied to program enrollment.

visa_types.name: 9(g) Pre-arranged Employment Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who have a pre-arranged employment offer in the Philippines and will engage in lawful occupation for wages, salary, or other compensation.
visa_types.notes: Two categories: Commercial (for regular company employment) and Non-Commercial (for missionary, social, or rehabilitation work). Validity options: 1, 2, or 3 years. Fees (as of 2014): PHP 10,130 principal (commercial, non-Top 1,000 corp, 1-year) plus USD 50 ACR I-Card fee. Top 1,000 Corporation and non-commercial rates vary. Employer must provide notarized certification of foreign and Filipino employee count. Process involves pre-screening, fee payment, hearing, biometric capture, and passport submission.

visa_types.name: 9(d) Treaty Trader or Treaty Investor Visa
visa_types.purpose: For nationals of the USA, Japan, and Germany to carry on trade or commerce pursuant to an existing bilateral treaty or to develop and direct operations of a business enterprise in the Philippines.
visa_types.notes: Available only to nationals of USA, Japan, and Germany under existing treaties of commerce and navigation. Validity options: 1 year (PHP 9,620 principal) or 2 years (PHP 13,650 principal) plus ACR I-Card fee of USD 50/year. No minimum investment amount specified. Renewable. Dependents (spouse, children) may be included with separate fees.

visa_types.name: 9(e) Accredited Official of Foreign Government Visa
visa_types.purpose: For accredited officials of foreign governments and international organizations on assignment to the Philippines.
visa_types.notes: Issued to diplomats, consular officers, and officials of accredited international organizations. Granted in coordination with the Department of Foreign Affairs. Specific fee structure and duration tied to assignment term.

visa_types.name: Special Resident Retiree's Visa (SRRV)
visa_types.purpose: A special non-immigrant visa for foreign retirees allowing indefinite stay in the Philippines as long as the required time deposit or investment is maintained.
visa_types.notes: Issued by Bureau of Immigration through the Philippine Retirement Authority (PRA). Multiple sub-types: SRRV Classic (age 50+, pensioner: USD 15,000 deposit; non-pensioner: USD 30,000; age 40-49: higher thresholds), SRRV Courtesy (age 50+, pensioner: USD 1,500; lower thresholds for former Filipinos). Minimum age: 40 years. Multiple-entry, indefinite stay while investment maintained. Holders may retain original citizenship. No documented path to Philippine citizenship through SRRV. Contact: Philippine Retirement Authority, +632 8848 1412.

visa_types.name: Special Investor's Resident Visa (SIRV)
visa_types.purpose: For qualified foreign investors remitting funds to establish or participate in a business enterprise in the Philippines under Board of Investments priority areas.
visa_types.notes: Requires remittance of at least USD 75,000 into a Peso Time Deposit at Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) or Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP). Funds must remain on deposit minimum 30 days, maximum 180 days. Investment must be made within 180 days of probationary visa issuance. Probationary SIRV valid 6 months; Indefinite SIRV valid indefinitely while investment maintained. Minimum age: 21 years. Apply at BOI One-Stop SIRV Center, 2nd Floor, BOI, #385 Senator Gil J. Puyat Avenue, Makati City.

visa_types.name: Temporary Resident Visa (TRV)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals married to Filipino citizens or under specific bilateral arrangements, allowing temporary residence in the Philippines.
visa_types.notes: Primarily for foreign spouses of Filipino citizens from countries without reciprocity agreements, and Indian nationals under a special arrangement. Conversion fee: PHP 8,620 per person (general), PHP 37,060 for Indian nationals. Extension fee: PHP 13,100 per person. Indian nationals may receive 5-year validity extensions. ACR I-Card required at additional USD 50/year. Passport surrendered during processing.

visa_types.name: Immigrant Visa by Marriage (13A)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals married to Filipino citizens to obtain permanent immigrant residence in the Philippines.
visa_types.notes: Grants permanent resident status to foreign spouses of Filipino citizens. Requires valid marriage to a Filipino citizen. Apply at Bureau of Immigration Main Office. Initial probationary period typically required before indefinite status granted.

visa_types.name: Permanent Resident Visa (PRV) / Returning Resident (13E/13G)
visa_types.purpose: For returning former residents and former natural-born Filipino citizens who wish to re-establish permanent residence in the Philippines.
visa_types.notes: 13E: Returning Resident for those who previously held Philippine permanent residency. 13G: Returning former natural-born Filipino citizens who have been naturalized abroad. Apply at Bureau of Immigration.

visa_types.name: Special Visa for Employment Generation (SVEG)
visa_types.purpose: For qualified foreign nationals who will establish and operate a business enterprise in the Philippines that creates employment for at least 10 Filipino workers.
visa_types.notes: Requires applicant to employ at least 10 Filipinos in a lawful and sustainable enterprise. Fee: PHP 29,330 per person (principal, spouse, and each dependent child) plus USD 250 ACR I-Card fee. Fees last updated March 2014; verify current rates with BI. Apply at Bureau of Immigration Main Office. Process involves pre-screening, payment, hearing, biometric capture, and visa implementation.

visa_types.name: Digital Nomad Visa (DNV)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who work remotely via digital technologies exclusively for overseas clients or employers and wish to reside temporarily in the Philippines.
visa_types.notes: Authorized by Executive Order No. 86, s. 2025 (signed April 24, 2025). Allows stay up to 1 year, renewable for another year. Eligibility: age 18+, works remotely for overseas clients/employers, from countries offering reciprocal digital nomad visas to Filipinos, minimum income USD 24,000/year, valid health insurance, clean criminal record. Foreign-sourced income not subject to Philippine taxation. Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) by Bureau of Immigration were pending as of mid-2025; check immigration.gov.ph for current status.

visa_types.name: Special Visa Upon Arrival (SEVUA)
visa_types.purpose: For endorsed foreign investors, athletes, conference delegates, World Bank/ADB officials, and other special categories requiring expedited visa processing on arrival.
visa_types.notes: Not nationality-based; requires prior endorsement from relevant Philippine government agency (BOI, PRA, PCCI) or event organizer. Single-entry typically grants 30 days. Multiple-entry SEVUA available for multinational executives at Commissioner's discretion. Must be arranged before arrival through the endorsing government agency.
